1	# Unified Non-Level-2 Refresh With Legacy CLI Deprecation
     2	
     3	## Summary
     4	Refactor `quant-build-production-dataset` into the single canonical refresh entrypoint for all non-Level-2 data, and deprecate the old direct refresh/build CLIs that currently let users bypass the unified path. Default behavior remains **daily-first**: resolve the latest completed SSE trading day, refresh daily/reference data, build and gate execution-ready artifacts, and build/gate ML-ready only when the latest monthly boundary is complete.
     5	
     6	## Key Changes
     7	- Make `quant-build-production-dataset` the only default public refresh command:
     8	  - optional `--end-date`; omitted resolves to latest completed SSE open day <= today
     9	  - generated labels from `START/END`, with no hardcoded `20260424` orchestration paths
    10	  - `--dry-run` emits the full step plan, resolved dates, labels, and skip reasons
    11	  - `--strict-full` fails if ML-ready cannot be completed
    12	  - `--skip-baostock` and `--skip-gdelt-doc-api` opt out of slower diagnostics
    13	- Orchestrate all non-Level-2 families in dependency order:
    14	  - Tushare validation and latest-date resolution
    15	  - stock master/BaoStock/sentinels
    16	  - daily A-share store, ETF universe/store/reference
    17	  - benchmark constituents/returns
    18	  - fundamentals, PIT fundamentals, PIT industry, non-L2 reference
    19	  - regimes, macro/events with GDELT diagnostics, noise layer
    20	  - execution-ready build/gate
    21	  - ML-ready build/gate only when monthly signal plus next rebalance-day data exists
    22	- Add a shared internal orchestration layer so the unified command does not shell out to deprecated public CLIs. Old CLI modules can call the shared step functions only after an explicit bypass flag.
    23	
    24	## Legacy CLI Deprecation
    25	- Deprecate direct data-refresh/build entrypoints by requiring `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly:
    26	  - `quant-build-monthly-history`, `quant-build-universe-history`, `quant-build-etf-universe`
    27	  - `quant-backfill-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-reference`
    28	  - `quant-backfill-fundamentals`, `quant-backfill-non-l2-reference`
    29	  - `quant-backfill-benchmark-constituents`, `quant-backfill-benchmark-returns`
    30	  - `quant-build-etf-proxy-returns`, `quant-build-regime-tags`, `quant-build-macro-event-layer`
    31	  - `quant-build-noise-layer`, `quant-build-ml-dataset`, `quant-build-fundamental-features`
    32	  - `quant-build-pit-industry`, `quant-build-baostock-reference`, `quant-build-baostock-sentinels`
    33	- Keep validation/gate commands active and not deprecated:
    34	  - `quant-validate-tushare`
    35	  - `quant-validate-store`
    36	  - `quant-gate-ml-dataset`
    37	  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset`
    38	  - `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset`
    39	- Direct deprecated CLIs should fail fast with:
    40	  - replacement command: `uv run quant-build-production-dataset`
    41	  - escape hatch: rerun with `--allow-manual-data-step`
    42	  - clear note that manual runs are for troubleshooting, diagnostics, or isolated reruns only
    43	- Update `AGENTS.md`, `docs/60_deprecation_inventory.md`, and current preprocessing docs so the unified command is the only documented default route.
    44	
    45	## Public Interface
    46	```bash
    47	uv run quant-build-production-dataset
    48	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --end-date 20260501
    49	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --dry-run
    50	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --strict-full
    51	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-baostock
    52	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-gdelt-doc-api
    53	```
    54	
    55	Output JSON includes resolved `daily_end_date`, `requested_end_date`, `monthly_boundary_status`, generated labels/paths, per-step statuses, execution-ready gate status, and ML-ready status.
    56	
    57	## Test Plan
    58	- Dry-run tests:
    59	  - generated plan contains no stale `20260424` paths for a new end date
    60	  - all non-Level-2 data families appear by default
    61	  - ML-ready is skipped with a clear reason when monthly boundary is incomplete
    62	- Deprecation tests:
    63	  - each deprecated direct CLI exits nonzero without `--allow-manual-data-step`
    64	  - deprecated direct CLIs run their existing behavior with the bypass flag
    65	  - gate/validation commands remain callable without bypass
    66	- Resolver and behavior tests:
    67	  - holiday/weekend resolves to prior completed open day
    68	  - `--strict-full` fails when ML-ready would be skipped
    69	  - daily-first mode can succeed with execution-ready gated and ML skipped
    70	- Quality gates:
    71	  ```bash
    72	  uv run ruff check src tests
    73	  uv run ruff format --check src tests
    74	  uv run pyright
    75	  uv run bandit -c pyproject.toml -r src/quantresearch
    76	  uv run --group test pytest -q
    77	  ```
    78	
    79	## Assumptions
    80	- “Deprecate” means direct manual CLIs remain available only behind `--allow-manual-data-step`, not removed from `pyproject.toml`.
    81	- `quant-build-production-dataset` remains the public command name.
    82	- Level-2 remains excluded.
    83	- Canonical research-run pointers are not updated automatically.
    84	- Unresolved questions: none.

1	# Unified Non-Level-2 Refresh With Legacy CLI Deprecation
     2	
     3	## Summary
     4	Refactor `quant-build-production-dataset` into the single canonical refresh entrypoint for all non-Level-2 data, and deprecate the old direct refresh/build CLIs that currently let users bypass the unified path. Default behavior remains **daily-first**: resolve the latest completed SSE trading day, refresh daily/reference data, build and gate execution-ready artifacts, and build/gate ML-ready only when the latest monthly boundary is complete.
     5	
     6	## Key Changes
     7	- Make `quant-build-production-dataset` the only default public refresh command:
     8	  - optional `--end-date`; omitted resolves to the latest completed SSE open day using Asia/Shanghai time, the SSE trading calendar, and an after-close cutoff
     9	  - generated labels and all derived paths come from a single `RefreshContext`, with no hardcoded `20260424` orchestration paths or downstream builder defaults
    10	  - `--dry-run` emits the full step plan, resolved dates, labels, explicit input/output paths, input hashes when files already exist, skip reasons, and checkpoint reuse decisions
    11	  - `--strict-full` fails if ML-ready cannot be completed
    12	  - `--mode daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh` makes operator intent explicit while keeping one public entrypoint
    13	  - `--skip-baostock` reuses existing audited BaoStock/patch artifacts only; it fails if required active-identity artifacts are absent and records freshness/status
    14	  - `--skip-gdelt-doc-api` skips slower GDELT document diagnostics only, while recording skipped status and any reused macro/event inputs
    15	- Orchestrate all non-Level-2 families in dependency order:
    16	  - Tushare validation, endpoint freshness probes, and latest-date resolution
    17	  - stock master/BaoStock/sentinels
    18	  - daily A-share store, ETF universe/store/reference
    19	  - benchmark constituents/returns
    20	  - fundamentals, PIT fundamentals, official industry manifest discovery/build, PIT industry, non-L2 reference
    21	  - regimes, macro/events with GDELT diagnostics, noise layer
    22	  - execution-ready build/gate
    23	  - ML-ready build/gate only when monthly signal plus next rebalance-day data exists
    24	- Add a shared internal orchestration layer so the unified command does not shell out to deprecated public CLIs. Old CLI modules can call the shared step functions only after an explicit bypass flag.
    25	- Add checkpoint/resume semantics before broad deprecation lands:
    26	  - stable step IDs
    27	  - started/completed timestamps
    28	  - normalized inputs, input hashes, output paths, and output hashes
    29	  - `rebuilt`, `reused_existing`, `skipped`, `stale`, `failed`, and `gated` statuses
    30	  - resume decisions based on matching context labels, paths, hashes, and step versions
    31	
    32	## Refresh Context
    33	- Introduce one immutable `RefreshContext` created by `quant-build-production-dataset` before any step runs:
    34	  - `start_date`
    35	  - `requested_end_date`
    36	  - `daily_end_date`
    37	  - `monthly_signal_end`
    38	  - `latest_rebalance_data_date`
    39	  - generated label set for daily, monthly, execution-ready, ML-ready, benchmark, ETF proxy, regime, macro/event, PIT fundamentals, PIT industry, and non-L2 reference artifacts
    40	  - all derived input/output paths for every builder and gate
    41	  - command mode, skip flags, bypass policy, and checkpoint location
    42	- Every orchestrated step must receive explicit context paths and labels. No step may rely on its standalone CLI defaults for production orchestration.
    43	- Manifest output must record the actual input paths, input hashes, output paths, output hashes, generated labels, source date coverage, source staleness, and whether each artifact was rebuilt or reused.
    44	- Production manifests and tests must verify recorded paths and hashes use the generated label for the requested refresh, not stale defaults from `20260424` or any other prior run.
    45	
    46	## Date Resolution and Freshness
    47	- Date resolution uses Asia/Shanghai local date/time and the SSE official trading calendar.
    48	- If `--end-date` is omitted:
    49	  - before the configured market-data completion cutoff, use the latest completed SSE open day strictly before today
    50	  - after the cutoff, use today only if today is an SSE open day and required endpoint freshness probes confirm complete data
    51	  - on weekends and SSE holidays, snap backward to the prior completed SSE open day
    52	- If `--end-date` is provided:
    53	  - preserve it as `requested_end_date`
    54	  - set `daily_end_date` to that date only when it is an open day with complete endpoint freshness
    55	  - otherwise snap `daily_end_date` backward to the prior completed open day and record `requested_end_date_status=closed_or_incomplete`
    56	  - fail only when `--strict-full` or an explicit future date requires data that cannot be available
    57	- The resolver must include a fixture where `today=2026-05-02` resolves `daily_end_date=20260430`, because SSE lists Labor Day closure from May 1 through May 5, 2026 and reopening on May 6.
    58	- Extend Tushare validation beyond stock-basic access. Freshness probes must cover daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limit data, ETF daily, fund adjustment, fund NAV, benchmark data, and configured non-Level-2 families before the context is allowed to run live steps.
    59	
    60	## Legacy CLI Deprecation
    61	- Deprecate direct data-refresh/build entrypoints by requiring `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly:
    62	  - `quant-build-monthly-history`, `quant-build-universe-history`, `quant-build-etf-universe`
    63	  - `quant-backfill-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-reference`
    64	  - `quant-backfill-fundamentals`, `quant-backfill-non-l2-reference`
    65	  - `quant-backfill-benchmark-constituents`, `quant-backfill-benchmark-returns`
    66	  - `quant-build-etf-proxy-returns`, `quant-build-regime-tags`, `quant-build-macro-event-layer`
    67	  - `quant-build-noise-layer`, `quant-build-ml-dataset`, `quant-build-fundamental-features`
    68	  - `quant-build-pit-industry`, `quant-build-official-industry-manifest`
    69	  - `quant-build-baostock-reference`, `quant-build-baostock-sentinels`
    70	  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset`
    71	- Keep validation/gate commands active and not deprecated:
    72	  - `quant-validate-tushare`
    73	  - `quant-validate-store`
    74	  - `quant-gate-ml-dataset`
    75	  - `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset`
    76	- Direct deprecated CLIs should fail fast with:
    77	  - replacement command: `uv run quant-build-production-dataset`
    78	  - escape hatch: rerun with `--allow-manual-data-step`
    79	  - clear note that manual runs are for troubleshooting, diagnostics, or isolated reruns only
    80	- `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` is a builder, so it is internal/orchestrator-only by default and requires `--allow-manual-data-step` when invoked directly. `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset` remains unrestricted.
    81	- Manual bypass runs must preserve current standalone behavior only when real arguments are provided explicitly. Bypass mode must not silently use stale production defaults.
    82	- `quant-build-official-industry-manifest` is part of the unified orchestration because PIT industry consumes its source manifest. If an operator bypass reuses an existing manifest, output JSON must record manifest path, source period coverage, source staleness, and reuse status.
    83	- Update `AGENTS.md`, `docs/60_deprecation_inventory.md`, and current preprocessing docs so the unified command is the only documented default route.
    84	
    85	## Public Interface
    86	```bash
    87	uv run quant-build-production-dataset
    88	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --end-date 20260502
    89	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --dry-run
    90	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --strict-full
    91	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode daily-refresh
    92	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode monthly-refresh
    93	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode full-refresh
    94	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-baostock
    95	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-gdelt-doc-api
    96	```
    97	
    98	Output JSON includes `requested_end_date`, `daily_end_date`, `requested_end_date_status`, `monthly_signal_end`, `monthly_boundary_status`, command mode, generated labels/paths, per-step statuses, checkpoint decisions, skipped/reused/stale/failed/gated states, execution-ready gate status, and ML-ready status.
    99	
   100	For `--skip-baostock`, output JSON must still include the active stock-universe identity source, patched master path, audited patch source path, sentinel status, freshness, and whether the required artifacts were reused.
   101	
   102	## Test Plan
   103	- Dry-run tests:
   104	  - generated plan contains no stale `20260424` paths for a new end date
   105	  - all non-Level-2 data families appear by default
   106	  - generated plan includes official industry manifest build/discovery before PIT industry
   107	  - generated plan includes explicit generated inputs for production constants, ML dataset, execution-ready, ETF proxy, macro regimes, benchmark, labels, and gates
   108	  - ML-ready is skipped with a clear reason when monthly boundary is incomplete
   109	  - dry-run fixture covers a month-end-complete date
   110	  - dry-run fixture covers an incomplete monthly boundary
   111	- Deprecation tests:
   112	  - each deprecated direct CLI exits nonzero without `--allow-manual-data-step`
   113	  - deprecated direct CLIs run their existing behavior with the bypass flag
   114	  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` requires `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly
   115	  - bypass runs require explicit real arguments for production-sensitive paths and do not fall back to stale defaults
   116	  - gate/validation commands remain callable without bypass
   117	- Resolver and behavior tests:
   118	  - holiday/weekend resolves to prior completed open day
   119	  - `today=2026-05-02` resolves `daily_end_date=20260430`
   120	  - explicit closed `--end-date` preserves `requested_end_date`, snaps `daily_end_date` backward, and records the closed/incomplete status
   121	  - endpoint freshness probes block live execution when daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limits, ETF daily, fund_adj, fund_nav, benchmark, or non-Level-2 families are stale
   122	  - `--strict-full` fails when ML-ready would be skipped
   123	  - daily-first mode can succeed with execution-ready gated and ML skipped
   124	- Integration tests:
   125	  - production orchestration uses an internal API with stubbed steps instead of `uv run` subprocess command lists
   126	  - no deprecated console script names appear in the production execution path
   127	  - every step receives explicit paths from `RefreshContext`
   128	  - output schema distinguishes `skipped`, `reused_existing`, `stale`, `failed`, and `gated`
   129	  - partial daily-first success records execution-ready success and ML-ready skip without marking the full run failed
   130	  - manifest tests assert recorded input paths and hashes match the generated refresh label
   131	  - checkpoint/resume tests reuse only when step IDs, labels, paths, hashes, and step versions match
   132	- Quality gates:
   133	  ```bash
   134	  uv run ruff check src tests
   135	  uv run ruff format --check src tests
   136	  uv run pyright
   137	  uv run bandit -c pyproject.toml -r src/quantresearch
   138	  uv run --group test pytest -q
   139	  ```
   140	
   141	## Assumptions
   142	- “Deprecate” means direct manual CLIs remain available only behind `--allow-manual-data-step`, not removed from `pyproject.toml`.
   143	- `quant-build-production-dataset` remains the public command name.
   144	- Level-2 remains excluded.
   145	- Canonical research-run pointers are not updated automatically.
   146	- Unresolved questions: none.

# Unified Non-Level-2 Refresh With Legacy CLI Deprecation

## Summary
Refactor `quant-build-production-dataset` into the single canonical refresh entrypoint for all non-Level-2 data, and deprecate the old direct refresh/build CLIs that currently let users bypass the unified path. Default behavior remains **daily-first**: resolve the latest completed SSE trading day, refresh daily/reference data, build and gate execution-ready artifacts, and build/gate ML-ready only when the latest monthly boundary is complete.

## Key Changes
- Make `quant-build-production-dataset` the only default public refresh command:
  - optional `--end-date`; omitted resolves to the latest completed SSE open day using Asia/Shanghai time, the SSE trading calendar, and an after-close cutoff
  - generated labels and all derived paths come from a single `RefreshContext`, with no hardcoded `20260424` orchestration paths or downstream builder defaults
  - `--dry-run` emits the full step plan, resolved dates, labels, explicit input/output paths, input hashes when files already exist, skip reasons, and checkpoint reuse decisions
  - `--strict-full` fails if ML-ready cannot be completed
  - `--mode daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh` makes operator intent explicit while keeping one public entrypoint
  - `--skip-baostock` reuses existing audited BaoStock/patch artifacts only; it fails if required active-identity artifacts are absent and records freshness/status
  - `--skip-gdelt-doc-api` skips slower GDELT document diagnostics only, while recording skipped status and any reused macro/event inputs
- Orchestrate all non-Level-2 families in dependency order:
  - Tushare validation, endpoint freshness probes, and latest-date resolution
  - stock master/BaoStock/sentinels
  - daily A-share store, ETF universe/store/reference
  - benchmark constituents/returns
  - fundamentals, PIT fundamentals, official industry manifest discovery/build, PIT industry, non-L2 reference
  - regimes, macro/events with GDELT diagnostics, noise layer
  - execution-ready build/gate
  - ML-ready build/gate only when monthly signal plus next rebalance-day data exists
- Add a shared internal orchestration layer so the unified command does not shell out to deprecated public CLIs. Old CLI modules can call the shared step functions only after an explicit bypass flag.
- Add checkpoint/resume semantics before broad deprecation lands:
  - stable step IDs
  - started/completed timestamps
  - normalized inputs, input hashes, output paths, and output hashes
  - `rebuilt`, `reused_existing`, `skipped`, `stale`, `failed`, and `gated` statuses
  - resume decisions based on matching context labels, paths, hashes, and step versions

## Refresh Context
- Introduce one immutable `RefreshContext` created by `quant-build-production-dataset` before any step runs:
  - `start_date`
  - `requested_end_date`
  - `daily_end_date`
  - `monthly_signal_end`
  - `latest_rebalance_data_date`
  - generated label set for daily, monthly, execution-ready, ML-ready, benchmark, ETF proxy, regime, macro/event, PIT fundamentals, PIT industry, and non-L2 reference artifacts
  - all derived input/output paths for every builder and gate
  - command mode, skip flags, bypass policy, and checkpoint location
- Every orchestrated step must receive explicit context paths and labels. No step may rely on its standalone CLI defaults for production orchestration.
- Manifest output must record the actual input paths, input hashes, output paths, output hashes, generated labels, source date coverage, source staleness, and whether each artifact was rebuilt or reused.
- Production manifests and tests must verify recorded paths and hashes use the generated label for the requested refresh, not stale defaults from `20260424` or any other prior run.

## Date Resolution and Freshness
- Date resolution uses Asia/Shanghai local date/time and the SSE official trading calendar.
- If `--end-date` is omitted:
  - before the configured market-data completion cutoff, use the latest completed SSE open day strictly before today
  - after the cutoff, use today only if today is an SSE open day and required endpoint freshness probes confirm complete data
  - on weekends and SSE holidays, snap backward to the prior completed SSE open day
- If `--end-date` is provided:
  - preserve it as `requested_end_date`
  - set `daily_end_date` to that date only when it is an open day with complete endpoint freshness
  - otherwise snap `daily_end_date` backward to the prior completed open day and record `requested_end_date_status=closed_or_incomplete`
  - fail only when `--strict-full` or an explicit future date requires data that cannot be available
- The resolver must include a fixture where `today=2026-05-02` resolves `daily_end_date=20260430`, because SSE lists Labor Day closure from May 1 through May 5, 2026 and reopening on May 6.
- Extend Tushare validation beyond stock-basic access. Freshness probes must cover daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limit data, ETF daily, fund adjustment, fund NAV, benchmark data, and configured non-Level-2 families before the context is allowed to run live steps.

## Legacy CLI Deprecation
- Deprecate direct data-refresh/build entrypoints by requiring `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly:
  - `quant-build-monthly-history`, `quant-build-universe-history`, `quant-build-etf-universe`
  - `quant-backfill-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-reference`
  - `quant-backfill-fundamentals`, `quant-backfill-non-l2-reference`
  - `quant-backfill-benchmark-constituents`, `quant-backfill-benchmark-returns`
  - `quant-build-etf-proxy-returns`, `quant-build-regime-tags`, `quant-build-macro-event-layer`
  - `quant-build-noise-layer`, `quant-build-ml-dataset`, `quant-build-fundamental-features`
  - `quant-build-pit-industry`, `quant-build-official-industry-manifest`
  - `quant-build-baostock-reference`, `quant-build-baostock-sentinels`
  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset`
- Keep validation/gate commands active and not deprecated:
  - `quant-validate-tushare`
  - `quant-validate-store`
  - `quant-gate-ml-dataset`
  - `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset`
- Direct deprecated CLIs should fail fast with:
  - replacement command: `uv run quant-build-production-dataset`
  - escape hatch: rerun with `--allow-manual-data-step`
  - clear note that manual runs are for troubleshooting, diagnostics, or isolated reruns only
- `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` is a builder, so it is internal/orchestrator-only by default and requires `--allow-manual-data-step` when invoked directly. `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset` remains unrestricted.
- Manual bypass runs must preserve current standalone behavior only when real arguments are provided explicitly. Bypass mode must not silently use stale production defaults.
- `quant-build-official-industry-manifest` is part of the unified orchestration because PIT industry consumes its source manifest. If an operator bypass reuses an existing manifest, output JSON must record manifest path, source period coverage, source staleness, and reuse status.
- Update `AGENTS.md`, `docs/60_deprecation_inventory.md`, and current preprocessing docs so the unified command is the only documented default route.

## Public Interface
```bash
uv run quant-build-production-dataset
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --end-date 20260430
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --dry-run
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --strict-full
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode daily-refresh
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode monthly-refresh
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode full-refresh
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-baostock
uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-gdelt-doc-api
```

Output JSON includes `requested_end_date`, `daily_end_date`, `requested_end_date_status`, `monthly_signal_end`, `monthly_boundary_status`, command mode, generated labels/paths, per-step statuses, checkpoint decisions, skipped/reused/stale/failed/gated states, execution-ready gate status, and ML-ready status.

For `--skip-baostock`, output JSON must still include the active stock-universe identity source, patched master path, audited patch source path, sentinel status, freshness, and whether the required artifacts were reused.

## Test Plan
- Dry-run tests:
  - generated plan contains no stale `20260424` paths for a new end date
  - all non-Level-2 data families appear by default
  - generated plan includes official industry manifest build/discovery before PIT industry
  - generated plan includes explicit generated inputs for production constants, ML dataset, execution-ready, ETF proxy, macro regimes, benchmark, labels, and gates
  - ML-ready is skipped with a clear reason when monthly boundary is incomplete
  - dry-run fixture covers a month-end-complete date
  - dry-run fixture covers an incomplete monthly boundary
- Deprecation tests:
  - each deprecated direct CLI exits nonzero without `--allow-manual-data-step`
  - deprecated direct CLIs run their existing behavior with the bypass flag
  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` requires `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly
  - bypass runs require explicit real arguments for production-sensitive paths and do not fall back to stale defaults
  - gate/validation commands remain callable without bypass
- Resolver and behavior tests:
  - holiday/weekend resolves to prior completed open day
  - `today=2026-05-02` resolves `daily_end_date=20260430`
  - explicit closed `--end-date` preserves `requested_end_date`, snaps `daily_end_date` backward, and records the closed/incomplete status
  - endpoint freshness probes block live execution when daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limits, ETF daily, fund_adj, fund_nav, benchmark, or non-Level-2 families are stale
  - `--strict-full` fails when ML-ready would be skipped
  - daily-first mode can succeed with execution-ready gated and ML skipped
- Integration tests:
  - production orchestration uses an internal API with stubbed steps instead of `uv run` subprocess command lists
  - no deprecated console script names appear in the production execution path
  - every step receives explicit paths from `RefreshContext`
  - output schema distinguishes `skipped`, `reused_existing`, `stale`, `failed`, and `gated`
  - partial daily-first success records execution-ready success and ML-ready skip without marking the full run failed
  - manifest tests assert recorded input paths and hashes match the generated refresh label
  - checkpoint/resume tests reuse only when step IDs, labels, paths, hashes, and step versions match
- Quality gates:
  ```bash
  uv run ruff check src tests
  uv run ruff format --check src tests
  uv run pyright
  uv run bandit -c pyproject.toml -r src/quantresearch
  uv run --group test pytest -q
  ```

## Assumptions
- “Deprecate” means direct manual CLIs remain available only behind `--allow-manual-data-step`, not removed from `pyproject.toml`.
- `quant-build-production-dataset` remains the public command name.
- Level-2 remains excluded.
- Canonical research-run pointers are not updated automatically.
- Unresolved questions: none.

What changed:

- Added a required `RefreshContext` covering dates, generated labels, all derived paths, skip policy, checkpoint location, and manifest inputs/hashes.

- Corrected the live-date example around the 2026 Labor Day closure. External check used: SSE 2026 closure page, https://www.sse.com.cn/disclosure/dealinstruc/closed/.
- Added official industry manifest orchestration/deprecation details.
- Defined `--skip-baostock` as reuse-only for audited BaoStock/patch artifacts, with required freshness/status reporting.
- Expanded tests for internal orchestration, stale default prevention, manifest path/hash validation, bypass behavior, partial success schema, endpoint freshness, and checkpoint/resume.
- Added the suggested checkpoint/resume contract and `daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh` mode posture.

Formal re-review result: all six findings and both additional optimizations are addressed in the plan text. No findings rejected. No code tests were run because this was a plan-file-only patch; validation was a full re-read of the patched Markdown plus targeted checks for the previously problematic terms and examples.","resolved_findings":["High: execution-ready builder bypass resolved by marking quant-build-execution-ready-dataset internal/orchestrator-only by default and requiring --allow-manual-data-step for direct runs.","High: generated-label under-scoping resolved by adding RefreshContext, explicit per-step paths, manifest path/hash requirements, and stale-default tests across production, ML, execution-ready, ETF proxy, regimes, benchmark, labels, and gates.","High: latest completed trading-day semantics resolved with timezone, cutoff, holiday/weekend snapping, explicit closed-date behavior, endpoint freshness probes, and the 2026-05-02 -> 20260430 fixture.","Medium: PIT industry orchestration gap resolved by adding quant-build-official-industry-manifest to unified orchestration and deprecation policy, with manifest coverage/staleness recording.","Medium: --skip-baostock ambiguity resolved by defining it as reuse-only for existing audited BaoStock/patch artifacts, with failure on missing required artifacts and explicit status fields.","Medium: integration test gaps resolved by adding tests for internal orchestration APIs, no deprecated console scripts in production execution path, explicit RefreshContext inputs, output status schema, partial daily-first success, manifest path/hash checks, and bypass argument behavior.","Additional optimization: checkpoint/resume semantics added with stable step IDs, hashes, timestamps, statuses, and reuse rules.","Additional optimization: command posture added through --mode daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh under the single public CLI."],"rejected_findings":[],"touched_files":["/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md"],"unresolved_questions":[]}
